questions.question: In experimental design, what is the primary goal of a control group?
options: A) To test the effect of the independent variable
B) To provide a baseline for comparison
C) To collect data on the dependent variable
D) To introduce noise into the experiment
questions.explanation: A control group is used as a baseline to compare the results of the experimental group and to isolate the effect of the independent variable.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using randomization in experimental design?
options: A) To increase the sample size
B) To reduce bias and increase generalizability
C) To minimize cost
D) To speed up the experiment
questions.explanation: Randomization helps to reduce bias and increase the generalizability of the results by ensuring that the experimental units are selected randomly.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key principle of experimental design?
options: A) Correlation does not imply causation
B) The law of large numbers
C) The central limit theorem
D) The null hypothesis
questions.explanation: Correlation does not imply causation is a key principle of experimental design, which reminds researchers to avoid assuming a causal relationship between variables based on correlation.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a pilot study in experimental design?
options: A) To test the feasibility of the experiment
B) To test the effect of the independent variable
C) To collect data on the dependent variable
D) To refine the experimental design
questions.explanation: A pilot study is used to test the feasibility of the experiment, to identify potential issues, and to refine the experimental design before conducting the main experiment.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a confounding variable in experimental design?
options: A) The independent variable
B) The dependent variable
C) The control group
D) Age and gender of the participants
questions.explanation: Age and gender of the participants are examples of confounding variables, as they can affect the outcome of the experiment and must be controlled for in order to isolate the effect of the independent variable.
